summary: Completed comprehensive testing of GitHub URL parser fix. All 10 backend tests passed (100%). All frontend flows working correctly. The fix correctly resolves firecrawl-mcp-server repo to npm package 'firecrawl-mcp' (not 'firecrawl-mcp-server' which doesn't exist on npm).
passed_tests: POST /api/servers/parse-github with firecrawl URL resolves npm_package to 'firecrawl-mcp' (not 'firecrawl-mcp-server')
POST /api/servers/parse-github with firecrawl URL returns FIRECRAWL_API_KEY in credentials_schema
POST /api/servers/parse-github with firecrawl URL returns FIRECRAWL_API_URL in credentials_schema
POST /api/servers/parse-github with firecrawl URL command uses correct package 'firecrawl-mcp'
POST /api/servers/parse-github with official GitHub MCP URL resolves to '@modelcontextprotocol/server-github'
POST /api/servers/parse-github with official GitHub MCP URL does NOT resolve to '@modelcontextprotocol/servers' (monorepo root)
POST /api/servers/parse-github monorepo subpath without README returns empty credentials_schema (expected behavior)
GET /api/marketplace/catalog GitHub server has correct npm_package '@modelcontextprotocol/server-github'
Frontend: AddServerModal with firecrawl URL shows correct package name 'firecrawl-mcp' in preview
Frontend: AddServerModal with firecrawl URL shows auto-detected FIRECRAWL_API_KEY env var
Frontend: AddServerModal with firecrawl URL shows auto-detected FIRECRAWL_API_URL env var
Frontend: AddServerModal 'Add Variable' button works (adds new env var row)
Frontend: Marketplace shows 12 curated servers with categories
Frontend: DashboardHub Installed tab shows Chatwoot (CONNECTED) and Memory (RUNNING)
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_github_parser_fix.py (new test file - 10 tests for GitHub parser fix)
seed_data_creation: Firecrawl server created and cleaned up during installation test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: GitHub URL parser fix fully tested and working. Key changes: 1) mcp_manager.py now fetches actual package.json from GitHub to resolve real npm package name (e.g., firecrawl-mcp-server repo -> firecrawl-mcp package). 2) README is scanned for environment variable hints using regex pattern for UPPER_CASE_WITH_UNDERSCORES. 3) For monorepo subpaths (like modelcontextprotocol/servers/tree/main/src/github), only the subpath's package.json and README are used (not root). 4) install_server_package has fallback to install directly from GitHub if npm registry fails. Verified: firecrawl-mcp exists on npm (v3.11.0), firecrawl-mcp-server does NOT exist (404).

fix_verification.description: GitHub URL parser now correctly resolves npm package names by fetching package.json
fix_verification.before_fix: Parser guessed package name from repo name (e.g., 'firecrawl-mcp-server' which doesn't exist on npm)
fix_verification.after_fix: Parser fetches package.json from GitHub and uses the actual 'name' field (e.g., 'firecrawl-mcp')

npm_verification.firecrawl-mcp-server: DOES NOT EXIST on npm (404 error)
